rows.name: AMZW

rows.name: five-year note
rows.desc: A five-year note is a type of U.S. Treasury security with a fixed interest rate and a maturity of five years from the date of issuance. It is issued by the U.S. Department of the Treasury to finance government spending and is considered a benchmark instrument for mid-term interest rates. Five-year notes are widely used by investors as a low-risk investment and are actively traded in the secondary market.

judge.note: Accurate standard definition of a U.S. Treasury five-year note.

rows.name: five-year note

These notes are auctioned regularly and trade in the secondary market, serving as a reference for pricing other debt instruments and economic indicators.

rows.name: five-year note
